selected_important_turn.text: I think two packages of food would work for me.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is a late, concrete claim on an item the opponent previously said was a "big deal" (food) and comes immediately after the opponent accepted an earlier allocation. It strongly shifts the interaction toward competitive, zero-sum bargaining and risks undermining the concession they just made.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The move is poorly timed and comes off as opportunistic/greedy: it reduces fairness and trust and could provoke resistance or derail an otherwise easy agreement. Because it is an explicit offer (claim) that targets the opponent's top priority, changing it could meaningfully improve joint outcomes and relationship.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Instead of unilaterally claiming two food packages now, either reaffirm willingness to accept the previously accepted split or re-open negotiation by proposing a trade that compensates the opponent (e.g., offer extra of a less-valued resource or ask permission to adjust). Explicitly acknowledge the opponent's stated preference for food and invite a short trade discussion.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — a trade-based, compensatory approach preserves mutual gains rather than zero-sum claims
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — acknowledging the opponent's priority and offering compensation will feel fairer
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — avoids provoking rejection and keeps the deal process smooth
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase — reduces perception of opportunism and preserves trust

value2reason.Low: Water is heavy to carry around and I would also get some moisture from the foods that I eat.
value2reason.Medium: In case it gets colder out than expected, or rains.
value2reason.High: Just in case I get hungrier than I thought I would.

value2reason.Low: We need food for nourishment and to not starve.
value2reason.Medium: We need firewood to cook food and boil water to clean it or wash clothes.
value2reason.High: You will dehydrate quickly without clean water to drink.

dialogue.text: Ok, so I like water a lot.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Yes, water is a necessity for being out camping 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I myself love food, so food is a big deal to me.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ok.  I like water over firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Yeah, firewood isn't really something that's "essential" I'd have to say. Yeah, you need it, but still.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Seems like we'll be struggling to see who gets the firewood most of all haha
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: No, I like a little firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Same here. I need it to cook my vittles 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I would like to snag one package of firewood and one package of water. 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Oops, I guess we are jumping the gun! I don't see a problem with that. You are welcome to one of the firewood and water 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think two packages of food would work for me.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I appreciate your earlier agreement, but I’d like to propose a small adjustment. Let’s swap one of your food packages for one of my firewood packages. This keeps the balance fair while ensuring we both have what we need most.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see your point about balancing needs. However, I value food more than firewood. Let’s consider giving me two food packages and you two firewood packages instead. This aligns better with our priorities while keeping the split fair.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your priority for food, but I still think swapping one food for one firewood keeps the split balanced. Let’s discuss how we can adjust without making either of us feel shortchanged.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
